channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Report claims AI boom could keep Australia using coal and gas for longer
item.description: A new report concludes the AI-fuelled surge of power-hungry data centres across Australia is jeopardising the country's energy transition.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Wilson

media:group.media:description: The artificial intelligence boom requires power-hungry data centres.

item.title: London hits record-breaking 35C as unusual May heatwave bakes Europe
item.description: The British capital's previous hottest May day was set only one day prior, when the mercury topped 33.5C.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Morgan

media:group.media:description: Temperatures in London topped 35 degrees Celsius on Tuesday afternoon, local time.

item.title: CBA boss says reconsider applying CGT change to non-passive assets
item.description: The Commonwealth Bank boss speaks out about the government's tax changes.

media:group.media:description: Commonwealth Bank chief executive Matt Comyn.

item.title: Four dead, including two children, after train hits Belgian school bus
item.description: The train collided with a minibus, which was carrying nine people at the time, at a level crossing.

media:group.media:description: Seven children, a driver and another adult were aboard the bus at the time of the accident.

item.title: NACC boss Paul Brereton admits he contributed to own 'downfall'
item.description: The corruption watchdog boss, appearing during a tense grilling at Senate estimates, admits his conduct became a "distraction".
item.dc:creator: Ewa Staszewska

media:group.media:description: Outgoing NACC commissioner Paul Brereton has faced questions about his resignation.

item.title: Romanian man jailed for stealing from elderly Australian shoppers
item.description: The Romanian citizen, who illegally visited Australia as part of a global "distraction fraud" operation, has been jailed after fleecing thousands of dollars from elderly Australian shoppers.
item.dc:creator: Grace Crivellaro

media:group.media:description: Vasile Bombonel being extradited to NSW.

item.title: Bondi gunman remained on counter-terror radar in 2022
item.description: Naveed Akram and his father travelled to Uzbekistan around 2022 and a former terror hotspot in the Philippines shortly before committing the Bondi massacre.
item.dc:creator: Tom Lowrey and Sean Rubinsztein-Dunlop

media:group.media:description: Naveed Akram and his father were able to travel to Uzbekistan around 2022, despite being known to ASIO.

item.title: Man discovers Stolen Generations truth after decades believing he was adopted
item.description: Ricky Hampson spent most of his life believing he had been adopted before learning several years ago he is a survivor of the Stolen Generations. Today, he is calling for more action and truth-telling.
item.dc:creator: Lois Maskiell

media:group.media:description: Ricky Hampson learned several years ago that he is a survivor of the Stolen Generation.

item.title: Archibald Prize sitter calls out APY art collective mistreatment
item.description: Award-winning artist Iluwanti Ken says the withdrawal of funding and support over the "white hands on black art" scandal has left her feeling like a stranger in her own industry.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Story
item.category: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

media:group.media:description: Ken, in November 2025, at the launch of the second edition of the book, Traditional Healers of Central Australia: Ngangkaṟi, which includes the firsthand accounts of about 30 traditional healers.

item.title: Flights resume at training school after two killed in plane crash
item.description: An aviation school resumes flight operations at Adelaide's Parafield Airport four weeks after an instructor and a student onboard a twin-engine plane were killed when it crashed into a hangar.

media:group.media:description: The crash killed two people and caused a large blaze in a hangar.

item.title: Police officer accused of rape denied he was 'sleazy', court told
item.description: A police officer goes on trial in South Australia's District Court after pleading not guilty to two counts of rape and one count of attempted rape.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: The officer is standing trial in South Australia's District Court.

item.title: Navy chief says Chinese flotilla in Tasman Sea had 'impact'
item.description: Australia's Chief of Navy says China stirred "anger" in New Zealand when it sent a powerful naval flotilla into the Tasman Sea last year, encouraging political support on both sides of the ditch for increased defence spending.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Dziedzic and Tom Lowrey

media:group.media:description: Mark Hammond (right) speaking at the Indian Ocean Defence and Security conference in Perth.

item.title: More ISIS-linked women and children land in Melbourne and Sydney
item.description: Two flights carrying women and children linked to the Islamic State group have landed in Australia.

media:group.media:description: ISIS-linked Kirsty Rosse-Emile and children left Melbourne Airport.

item.title: Interim AVOs issued to keep developer away from council mayor and GM
item.description: A Southern Highlands property developer is only allowed to communicate with his local council's mayor and general manager through his lawyer under interim AVOs issued against him during a heated court appearance.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Chandler

media:group.media:description: Theo Onisforou, 68, pleaded not guilty to three offences and was handed two interim AVOs.

item.title: Motorbike rider, 14, dies after head-on collision with van
item.description: The teenage boy died in hospital after being involved in a head-on collision on a country road in north Queensland three days ago.
item.dc:creator: Owen Jacques

media:group.media:description: The 14-year-old boy was struck by an oncoming van while riding a motorbike.

item.title: Spirit ferries operator set for major restructure under Tasmanian Labor plan
item.description: The two government businesses responsible for the botched Spirit of Tasmania ship replacement program would undergo a major restructure, with one stripped of all assets, under a Tasmanian Labor government.
item.dc:creator: Josh Duggan

media:group.media:description: Labor's yet-to-be-named entity would take ownership of TT-Line's ships, berthing facilities, gantries and terminals, as well as its estimated debt of $1 billion.

item.title: 'Very scary and sad': Travellers in limbo after company enters liquidation
item.description: An Australian travel company selling heavily discounted international holidays has collapsed, leaving customers scrambling and their plans in disarray.
item.dc:creator: Kristy Sexton-McGrath

media:group.media:description: Mary Tait (right) had booked a trip to China through AVG Travels.

item.title: Teary council officer tells ICAC they 'should be ashamed of yourselves'
item.description: Roxanne Thornton accuses Independent Commission Against Corruption of "laying the boot in" after four days of questioning over the manipulation of the recruitment process at the council to secure jobs for friends and associates.
item.dc:creator: Nabil Al-Nashar

media:group.media:description: Roxanne Thornton broke down in tears during her fourth day of questioning.

item.title: One woman will make Japanese history by going on maternity leave
item.description: A Japanese mayor will take maternity leave this year, becoming the first in the country's history to do so while in office.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Martin

media:group.media:description: Shoko Kawata's maternity leave announcement has created significant interest in Japan.

item.title: Hypersonic missile attack a sign of Putin's 'bruised ego', expert says
item.description: Russia escalates attacks on Kyiv by using a hypersonic missile in one of its largest assaults on the Ukrainian capital since the start of the four-year war, and warns foreigners to leave the city ahead of further strikes.
item.dc:creator: Annika Burgess

media:group.media:description: An injured man with his dog takes cover in a shelter after a Russian strike on a Kyiv neighbourhood.

item.title: Iron ore processing project dumped by BHP for not being profitable enough
item.description: Internal BHP documents reveal how the mining giant planned to set up an iron ore processing facility in the Pilbara to cut carbon emissions before backing away from it entirely.
item.dc:creator: Angus Grigg, Alex McDonald, and Jade Toomey

media:group.media:description: Internal documents from BHP show the mining giant dumped plans for an iron ore processing plant in the Pilbara.

item.title: K-pop triumphs at American Music Awards as Pussycat Dolls lead noughties nostalgia
item.description: Taylor Swift led the night with eight nominations but didn't take home a single award, while 80s rock star Billy Idol was honoured with a Lifetime Achievement Award.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming and Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Kimberly Wyatt, Nicole Scherzinger and Ashley Roberts of The Pussycat Dolls whipped out some of their hits.

item.title: Fan in Australian jersey catches Aussie-hit homer in 15-year first
item.description: Curtis Mead hit two home runs for Washington against fellow Australian Travis Bazzana's Cleveland side as two Australian-born players faced off in an MLB game for the first time since 2017. But it was a man in an Australian supporters shirt who stole the show.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jack Seiple had probably hoped to see Travis Bazzana score for the Guardians.

item.title: Port Fairy pool to be demolished due to 'financial madness' of repair bill
item.description: The pool's shuttering has been met by community uproar, with some locals rallying to protest the closure and calling for its reopening.
item.dc:creator: Jean Bell

media:group.media:description: Belfast Aquatics in Port Fairy has been closed after mould and algae were found in the facility.

item.title: Kumanjayi White family 'heartbroken' no charges laid over custody death
item.description: The family of Kumanjayi White tell the ABC they are "heartbroken" that no charges will be laid over his death in custody, as Northern Territory Police confirm their criminal investigation has been concluded.
item.dc:creator: Elsie Lange and Grace Atta

media:group.media:description: Kumanjayi White's family have released a statement after hearing no charges will be laid over his death.
